data.title: Payoneer, Shift4 downgraded as KBW looks ahead to 2025 payments landscape
data.description:
Payoneer Global (NASDAQ:PAYO) shares slid 7.4% in Monday afternoon trading after Keefe, Bruyette & Woods downgraded the payments infrastructure platform to Market Perform from Outperform following the stock's 95% surge this year.

Analyst Sanjay Sakhrani noted that lower interest rates will likely limit the company's EBITDA margin expansion and EBITDA growth over the next couple of years. Another negative catalyst, he added, is "the threat of higher tariffs on China" -- which "could pose a risk given that roughly 35% of PAYO’s revenues are derived from [small businesses] in greater China."

Sakhrani also lowered rival Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) to Market Perform as a potential CEO transition could be an overhang on the stock over the near-term.

The analyst thinks Shift4's (NYSE:FOUR) new CEO will be current President Taylor Lauber, he wrote in a note, although it may take at least until the back half of next year for her to "settle in and earn investor confidence."

"To the extent the current CEO looks to be a seller of the company, we think a meaningful premium from current levels may be hard to attain on the back of an unsuccessful strategic sale process earlier this year as well as the increase in share price YTD."

(FOUR) shares edged down 0.2% at press time.

data.title: Vertex reports positive long-term data for Casgevy
data.description:
Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) presented positive long-term data for its gene therapy Casgevy and issued a business update on the product.

The data showed 93% of treated patients, or 39 out of 42, with sickle cell disease who were at least 16 months of follow-up were free from vaso-occlusive crises, or VOCs, for at least 12 consecutive months. The mean duration of VOC-free was 30.9 months, with a maximum of 59.6 months, according to a statement.

For patients with transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, or TDT, 98% of patients, or 53 out of 54, who were at least 16 months of follow-up achieved transfusion-independence for at least 12 consecutive months with a weighted average hemoglobin of at least 9 g/dL. The mean duration of transfusion independence was 34.5 months, with a maximum of 64.1 months.

The data was presented at the annual meeting of the American Society of Hematology, which is being held Dec. 7-10 in San Diego.

Vertex (VRTX) said that over 45 authorized treatment centers have been activated around the world to support the delivery of Casgevy, with more than 40 patients having had a first cell collection.

Vertex (VRTX) added it has received approval for a third manufacturing facility for the product with its partner Lonza (OTCPK:LZAGY) (OTCPK:LZAGF).

Casgevy was developed with CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP).

data.title: Five9 edges higher as activist added to board
data.description:
Five9 (NASDAQ:FIVN) ticked higher by 0.4% after the call-center software firm confirmed that it appointed an activist holder to the board.

Sagar Gupta, a portfolio manager at Anson Funds, was added to Five9's board, according to a statement on Monday. With the appointment of Gupta, Five9’s board will increase to 10 directors, nine of whom are independent.

"I have greatly benefited from my constructive engagement with Five9’s management and Board over the past several months, and look forward to working collaboratively with my fellow directors to help Five9 capitalize on the opportunity that lies ahead," Gupta said in the statement.

Anson in July built up a stake in Five9 and began pushing the company to sell itself. Legion Partners in October acquired a stake in Five9 and began seeking a board seat.

Five9 (FIVN) entered into a cooperation agreement with Anson Funds, agreeing to a customary standstill, voting and confidentiality commitments, among other provisions.

News of Gupta's appointment was first reported by Reuters on Sunday.

data.title: Dollar jittery as markets await CPI data-Currency recap
data.description:
The U.S. Dollar inched lower on Monday, with last week’s jobs report pointing to another Fed cut later this month. Although markets have priced in a quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week, investors still await the U.S. consumer price data, due on Wednesday.

Despite slipping lower, the U.S. dollar faced only minor losses, as it continues to be regarded as a safe haven after renewed tensions in the Middle East.

Dollar weekly moves and key drivers:

The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the dollar against a basket of six foreign currencies, fell 0.55% last week.

The rally in the dollar after Donald Trump’s presidential win seems to have slowed down, with markets expecting the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates once more next week.

According to data released on Friday, the U.S. job growth surged in November, but a rise in the unemployment rate to 4.2% pointed to an easing labour market that should allow the Fed to cut interest rates again this month.

Losses in dollar were contained as it retained its spot as a safe investment haven after heightened geopolitical tensions. Syrian President Bashar al-Assad fled the country over the weekend, ousted by rebels who have taken control of the capital, sparking fears of an escalated conflict in the Middle East.

The political instability in South Korea last week, sparked by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s short-lived attempt to impose martial law, also strengthened the dollar.

During the week, the 10-year yield (US10Y) fell six points to 4.15% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell nine points to 4.11%.

Currency Highlights: Major currency movements (Dec 2 to Dec 6)

Euro (EUR:USD)

+0.79%

Pound Sterling (GBP:USD)

+0.76%

Japanese Yen (JPY:USD)

+0.15%

Chinese Yuan (CNY:USD)

+0.36%

Swiss Franc (CHF:USD)

+1.02%

Australian Dollar (AUD:USD

-1.23%

Canadian Dollar (CAD:USD)

-0.70%

Euro (EUR:USD) was last up 0.09% against the dollar on Monday, after edging higher last week, with markets eagerly awaiting the latest rate decision for the European Central Bank on Thursday, its final policy meeting of the year. Traders expect the ECB to agree to another 25-bps rate cut, its fourth such cut this year.

The Japanese yen (JPY:USD) edged lower on Monday, after revised GDP data showed that Japan’s economy grew slightly more than expected.

The Chinese Yuan (CNY:USD) rose a little, after falling against the dollar as data showed that Chinese consumer inflation contracted more than expected in November.

Market reactions and analysis:

Citi Research indicated that the U.S. dollar (DXY) is poised to continue its rally as the calendar turns over to 2025, adding that a push to $115 is a possibility.

“We expect the USD rally to continue into Q1 2025, and potentially persist through the first half, bringing the USD into a new and higher range relative to the past two years,” Citi Research stated on Friday in an investor note.

Mizuho Bank strategist Vishnu Varathan highlighted several geopolitical events, including the recent collapse of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad's regime, as well as macroeconomic factors and trades related to Trump, all of which are giving markets additional momentum to remain bullish on the dollar.

Economic Indicators to watch:

Some of the main events to watch this week include the ECB policy meeting on Thursday, where traders expect a quarter point rate cut.

Another point of focus would be China’s annual closed-door Central Economic Work Conference for hints on more stimulus measures from China’s central bank.

On the domestic front, the main economic reports to watch out for this week include the Consumer Price Index for November, as well as wholesale inventories and import pricing data.

Markets will closely watch the November inflation data, as it will be an important indicator for the Federal Reserve ahead of an upcoming interest-rate decision.

Dollar-Based ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN).

data.title: Nvidia slips as China opens probe on anti-monopoly concerns: report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) shares slipped 2% on Monday as China's antitrust agency opened a probe into the Jensen-Huang led company on concerns it may have beached monopoly laws.
  • The State Administration for Market Regulation has opened the probe, according to China Central Television. The news was first reported domestically by Bloomberg.
  • “NVIDIA wins on merit, as reflected in our benchmark results and value to customers, and customers can choose whatever solution is best for them,” a company spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. “We work hard to provide the best products we can in every region and honor our commitments everywhere we do business. We are happy to answer any questions regulators may have about our business.”
  • Nvidia has tweaked its existing artificial intelligence accelerators for sale in China to comply with existing U.S. export control laws. Nvidia said on its most recent earnings call that it expects China to remain a "very competitive" environment, notably in the data center market.

(This story has been updated with a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.)

data.title: Equitable Holdings raised to Outperform at KBW on upbeat outlook
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analyst Ryan Krueger upgraded Equitable Holdings (NYSE:EQH) stock to Outperform from Market Perform in a recent note as the financial services company is on track to deliver its 2023-2027 financial targets, setting the stage for a favorable outlook.

The company's strong financial results, improved business mix and stable balance sheet all come as (NYSE:EQH) changes hands at a "relatively modest" 2025 price-to-earnings multiple of 6.5x, the note said.

KBW's Outperform rate compares with the SA Quant system rating of Hold and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

(EQH) slipped 0.5% in midday trading.

data.title: Torex Gold resumes operations at ELG underground mine in Mexico
data.description:
Torex Gold Resources (OTCPK:TORXF) +6% in Monday's trading after saying it resumed operations at the El Limón Guajes underground mine in Mexico, where three workers died from carbon monoxide exposure.

Torex (OTCPK:TORXF) said it also resumed underground development and construction activities at Media Luna, a nearby project that is approaching conclusion.

FY 2024 production guidance and the schedule for the Media Luna project were not affected by the incident, the company said.

Torex (OTCPK:TORXF) said it has begun an investigation to determine the cause of the accident, but early indications are that the carbon monoxide exposure was an isolated incident in an area of the mine that was being prepared for ventilation following a stope blast.

The new Media Luna project will be tied into the El Limón Guajes complex, and combined are expected to produce 450K-470K oz of gold this year.

data.title: Google rises as tech giant unveils 'mind-boggling' quantum computing chip
data.description:
• Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares rose on Monday as the tech giant unveiled what it has called a "mind-boggling" quantum computing chip.
  • The chip, known as Willow, is capable of performing calculations in five minutes that would take the world’s most powerful supercomputers 10 septillion years, the tech giant said in a study published in Nature. "Our results present device performance that, if scaled, could realize the operational requirements of large scale fault-tolerant quantum algorithms," Google researchers wrote in the study's abstract.
  • Quantum computing stocks were mixed on Monday following the news, as Rigetti Computing (RGTI) traded higher by more than 2%. Conversely, IonQ (IONQ), Qubit Computing (QUBT) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) were sharply lower in late morning trading.

data.title: Barnes & Noble Education rallies after management points to a narrowed focus
data.description:
Barnes & Noble Education (NYSE:BNED) reported revenue fell 1.4% year-over-year in FQ2 to $602.1 million. The revenue decline was primarily driven by a net decrease of 109 physical and virtual locations, many of which were closures of the company's underperforming stores, which has helped to improve overall profitability.

"Strong adoption of our First Day affordable access programs, exceptional retail execution supporting our client institutions, and a disciplined focus on operational efficiency are reflected in our outstanding results," stated CEO Jonathan Shar. He said the company is confident in its improving momentum and the opportunities ahead.

Looking ahead, Barnes & Noble Education (BNED) management is working on simplifying operations to better focus on its core physical and virtual bookstore businesses. Notably, go-forward savings from recently completed and in-progress initiatives are now estimated at over $20 million.

As for strategy, the company's budget goals continue to target a material improvement in fiscal year 2025 GAAP operating results and adjusted EBITDA versus last year. Based on current estimates from management of capital expenditures and significantly reduced interest costs from last fiscal year, BNED believes it can drive meaningful operating free cash flow, which will be used to further de-lever its balance sheet.

Shares of Barnes & Noble Education (BNED) rallied 6.2% in late Monday morning trading. The consumer discretionary stock is still down more than 90% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: TDb Split class A share consolidation
data.description:
• TDb Split (TSX:XTD:CA) has announced a Class A share consolidation, where the recent extension of the Co.'s termination date included a retraction right for Class A shareholders and Preferred shareholders, which resulted in more Preferred shares being tendered than Class A shares.
  • To restore balance in the number of shares outstanding for each class of shares, Class A shareholders will receive 0.50 Class A shares for each Class A share they hold.
  • The total intrinsic value of Class A shares will remain the same, but the net asset value per share will increase proportionally following the consolidation.
  • This adjustment will also result in a higher asset coverage ratio for Preferred shares.
  • The impact of the Class A share consolidation will be reflected in the next reported net asset value per unit as at Dec. 13, 2024.
